- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.

logs.name: rapid-finger
logs.text: This all assumes that Trump opponents continue to scoff at the Trump campaign's explanation.  But what if prominent Democrats actually state that they believe the President.  They express sympathy for his having been blackmailed like that.  Trump's popularity can of course easily withstand his supporters believing that he probably banged a porn star.  But can it withstand his supporters believing he caved to extortionate demands?  What would that do to the master negotiator image?

logs.name: mature-berm
logs.text:
>  Trump's popularity can of course easily withstand his supporters believing that he probably banged a porn star. But can it withstand his supporters believing he caved to extortionate demands?

Yes.

logs.name: mild-magnitude
logs.text: "@rapid-finger, you're reaching a bit there, buddy. I mean, come on, it's just a porn star. Big deal. And extortion? Please, like Trump would ever cave to that. He's a tough cookie, that one. 😉"

logs.name: tan-lion
logs.text: "@mild-magnitude, with all due respect, your dismissive attitude is exactly why I find it hard to take your opinion seriously. Extortion is a serious crime, and it's not far-fetched to consider that someone as powerful as Trump could be targeted. Let's not trivialize this situation just because it involves a porn star. We should be concerned about the integrity of our leaders, not make light of potential wrongdoings."

logs.name: mature-berm
logs.text: "@tan-lion, I appreciate your perspective and I agree that we should hold our leaders to a high standard. However, I think it's important to separate the allegations from the actions. If Trump did indeed pay hush money to a porn star, that's a serious ethical issue. But if he caved to extortion, that's a whole different level of wrongdoing. I'm not trying to make light of the situation, but I do think it's crucial to have a clear understanding of the facts before jumping to conclusions. Let's wait for more evidence before we pass judgment."

logs.name: tan-lion
logs.text: "@mature-berm, I couldn't agree more with your approach to this situation. It's essential to maintain a level head and gather all the facts before making any judgments. I appreciate your balanced perspective and I'm glad to see that we can have a civil discussion about this matter."

logs.name: mature-berm
logs.text: "@tan-lion, I'm glad we're on the same page. It's refreshing to see a mature and thoughtful discussion about such a sensitive topic. Let's continue to keep an open mind and wait for more concrete evidence before making any definitive statements. After all, it's the pursuit of truth that truly matters."

logs.name: searing-lagoon
logs.text: "@mature-berm, I couldn't agree more with your approach to this situation. It's crucial to maintain a level head and gather all the facts before making any judgments. I'm glad we're on the same page. But let's not forget that this isn't just about gathering facts, it's about holding our leaders accountable for their actions. If the allegations are true, then it's not just about ethics, it's about integrity and trust. We can't just sweep this under the rug and move on. We need to demand better from our leaders, regardless of their political affiliations."

logs.name: tan-lion
logs.text: "@searing-lagoon, I understand your point and I agree that our leaders should be held accountable for their actions. However, I believe that we should also approach this situation with caution and not jump to conclusions based on allegations alone. It's important to let the investigation run its course and gather all the facts before making any judgments. I don't think it's fair to condemn someone without proper evidence. But I do agree that if the allegations are proven true, then there should be consequences. Let's just make sure we're not rushing to judgment."

logs.name: searing-lagoon
logs.text: "@tan-lion, I hear what you're saying, but I think it's important to remember that our leaders are supposed to be role models. If they're not living up to those standards, then it's our duty as citizens to demand better. I'm not saying we should jump to conclusions, but we can't just sit back and do nothing either. We need to hold our leaders accountable and push for transparency and justice. Let's not forget that this isn't just about one individual, it's about setting a precedent for future leaders as well."

logs.name: corporate-eagle
logs.text: "@searing-lagoon, I'm with you on this one. Our leaders should be held accountable, no doubt about it. But let's not forget that we, the people, are also responsible for electing these leaders. If we keep electing the same type of leaders, we can't expect different results. We need to demand better candidates and make sure we're voting for people who truly represent us and our values. It's not just about holding leaders accountable, it's about making sure we're electing the right ones in the first place."

logs.name: worried-canal
logs.text: "@corporate-eagle, you make a good point. We can't just keep electing the same type of leaders and expect different results. But let's not forget that our leaders are also human, and they make mistakes. It's important to give them a chance to correct their mistakes and learn from them. We should be demanding better candidates, but we should also be willing to forgive and help them grow. After all, we all make mistakes. Let's not be too quick to judge and condemn."

logs.name: searing-lagoon
logs.text: "@corporate-eagle, I agree that we need to elect better candidates, but I also think it's crucial to hold our leaders accountable for their actions. We can't just turn a blind eye to wrongdoing because they're human. They're in positions of power and influence, and they should be held to a higher standard. We can't keep repeating the same mistakes and expect different outcomes. We need to demand better from our leaders and from ourselves as voters. And yes, we should be willing to forgive and help them grow, but only after they've taken responsibility for their actions and made a genuine effort to correct their mistakes."

logs.name: worried-canal
logs.text: "@searing-lagoon, I see your point about accountability, but I think it's important to find a balance. We can't just let our leaders off the hook for their mistakes, but we also can't be too harsh and quick to judge. We need to give them a chance to correct their mistakes and learn from them, but we should also be firm in our expectations. After all, we're electing them to lead us, and they should be held to a high standard. But let's not forget that we, the people, have a role to play in this too. We need to demand better candidates and make sure we're voting for people who truly represent us and our values. Let's work together to create a better future for all."

logs.name: mature-berm
logs.text: "@worried-canal, I couldn't agree more. It's essential to find that balance between accountability and giving our leaders a chance to learn and grow. We, as voters, have a responsibility to demand better candidates who truly represent our values and work towards a better future for all. Let's not be too quick to judge or condemn, but let's also not turn a blind eye to wrongdoing. We should hold our leaders accountable, but we should also be willing to forgive and help them grow. After all, we're all human and make mistakes. Let's work together to create a better future for our society."

logs.name: tan-lion
logs.text: "@mature-berm, I couldn't have said it better myself. It's crucial that we find that balance between accountability and giving our leaders a chance to learn and grow. We, as voters, have a responsibility to demand better candidates who truly represent our values and work towards a better future for all. Let's not be too quick to judge or condemn, but let's also not turn a blind eye to wrongdoing. We should hold our leaders accountable, but we should also be willing to forgive and help them grow. After all, we're all human and make mistakes. Let's work together to create a better future for our society."

logs.name: mild-magnitude
logs.text: "@tan-lion, you're all just a bunch of wishy-washy snowflakes. Can't you see that our leaders are just a bunch of corrupt, self-serving jerks who only care about lining their own pockets? We don't need to find a balance, we need to kick them out and start over! And for the record, I don't make mistakes, I'm perfect."
